name: Daniel Kahneman
bio: Daniel Kahneman was an Israeli-American psychologist best known for his work on the psychology of judgment and decision-making as well as behavioral economics, for which he was awarded the 2002 Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences together with Vernon L. Smith. Kahneman's published empirical findings challenge the assumption of human rationality prevailing in modern economic theory. Kahneman became known as the "grandfather of behavioral economics."

awards.name: APA Award for Distinguished Scientific Contributions to Psychology

Honorary doctorate from the Julius Maximilians University of Würzburg
honorary doctor of Paris Descartes University
honorary doctor of the University of Paris 1 Pantheon-Sorbonne
APA Award for Outstanding Lifetime Contributions to Psychology
Leontief Prize for Advancing the Frontiers of Economic Thought
Distinguished Fellow of the American Economic Association
Fellow of the Society of Experimental Psychologists
Fellow of the Cognitive Science Society
Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences
